title: ferray-core: no alignment flag or ALIGNED check
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy tracks whether array data is properly aligned for the dtype (NPY_ARRAY_ALIGNED flag). Misaligned access can cause UB on some architectures and performance loss on x86. ferray-core's ArrayFlags has no alignment tracking. Since Rust's type system generally ensures alignment for typed slices, this is less critical, but it matters for: (1) arrays constructed from raw pointers (from_shape_ptr), (2) f16/bf16 on platforms with strict alignment, (3) SIMD operations that require 16/32/64-byte alignment. At minimum, document the alignment invariants; ideally add an is_aligned() check.
